title: TC-DISCONNECT-001: Disconnect via Android Bluetooth Settings — App UI State on Return
steps: Precondition: Device (e.g., Sync 20) is paired and connected to phone. Poly Studio Mobile app is open on Device screen showing 'Connected' status.
1. Navigate to: Android Settings → Bluetooth → Paired Devices → Tap gear icon next to [Device Name].
2. Tap 'Disconnect'. Verify device disconnects (Bluetooth settings show 'Disconnected').
3. Immediately return to Poly Studio Mobile app (do NOT force-kill).
4. Observe the device connection status on the main Device screen.
5. Tap into the device detail screen and observe the connection indicator.
expected: 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE VALIDATION: App must show 'Disconnected' or 'Tap to Connect' status. Actual (If Bug): App incorrectly shows 'Connected' (stale UI) because it missed the ACTION_ACL_DISCONNECTED broadcast while the user was in Bluetooth settings. The device detail screen must also reflect disconnected state. No phantom 'Connected' badge or green indicator should persist.

title: TC-DISCONNECT-002: Disconnect via Bluetooth Settings While App is Backgrounded — Foreground Resume Refresh
steps: Precondition: Device connected. App open on Device screen showing 'Connected'.
1. Press Home button to background the Poly Studio Mobile app.
2. Navigate to: Android Settings → Bluetooth → Paired Devices → Tap gear icon → 'Disconnect'.
3. Wait 5 seconds after disconnect completes.
4. Tap the Poly Studio Mobile app icon to bring it to foreground (not from Recents — from launcher to trigger full lifecycle resume).
5. Observe the connection status on the main Device screen.
6. Tap into device detail screen and verify all audio controls (volume, ANC, EQ) are disabled/greyed out.
expected: 🧠 DEEP LIFECYCLE: App must refresh state on resume from background. Connection status must show 'Disconnected'. All audio controls must be disabled/greyed out. Actual (If Bug): App shows stale 'Connected' state with active controls because onResume() did not trigger a Bluetooth state re-sync. 🧠 STATE TRIGGER GAP: This validates that the app correctly processes the disconnect callback even when the broadcast arrives while the app is in the BACKGROUND.

title: TC-DISCONNECT-003: Disconnect → Reconnect Cycle via Bluetooth Settings — State Consistency
steps: Precondition: Device connected. App open on Device screen.
1. Navigate to: Android Settings → Bluetooth → Paired Devices → Tap gear icon → 'Disconnect'.
2. Return to app. Verify 'Disconnected' status.
3. Navigate back to: Android Settings → Bluetooth → Paired Devices → Tap device name to reconnect.
4. Wait for connection to establish (Bluetooth settings show 'Connected').
5. Return to app. Observe connection status.
6. Repeat steps 1-5 for 3 cycles.
expected: 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE VALIDATION: After each disconnect, app must show 'Disconnected'. After each reconnect, app must show 'Connected' with all audio controls functional. No cumulative state drift across cycles. Actual (If Bug): After 2+ cycles, app may show incorrect state (e.g., shows Connected when actually disconnected, or vice versa) due to missed or duplicate broadcast processing.

title: TC-DISCONNECT-004: Disconnect via Bluetooth Settings During Active Media Playback — App Behavior
steps: Precondition: Device connected. Media (music/podcast) playing through the Poly device.
1. While media is actively playing, navigate to: Android Settings → Bluetooth → Paired Devices → Tap gear icon → 'Disconnect'.
2. Observe audio routing (audio should switch to phone speaker).
3. Return to Poly Studio Mobile app.
4. Observe connection status.
5. Tap Play on media app and verify audio routing.
expected: 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE VALIDATION: App must show 'Disconnected' status. Audio must route to phone speaker immediately upon disconnect. Actual (If Bug): App shows 'Connected' while audio is already on phone speaker — stale UI. Additionally, if user taps Play, audio should NOT route back to the disconnected Poly device. The app must not attempt to re-establish a connection automatically without explicit user action.

title: TC-DISCONNECT-005: Device Asymmetry — Disconnect Across Poly Device Families (Sync 20 vs. Voyager Free 60 vs. Voyager Legend 50)
steps: Precondition: Run this test separately for each device: [Sync 20, Voyager Free 60, Voyager Legend 50]. Each device must be paired and connected.
1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app. Verify device shows 'Connected'.
2. Navigate to: Android Settings → Bluetooth → Paired Devices → Tap gear icon → 'Disconnect'.
3. Return to app. Observe connection status.
4. Wait 30 seconds. Observe if app auto-reconnects or remains disconnected.
5. Manually reconnect via Bluetooth settings. Return to app. Verify state.
expected: 🧠 DEVICE ASYMMETRY GAP: All three device families must show 'Disconnected' immediately upon return to app. However, expected differences: Sync 20 (HFP only) may show disconnect faster than Voyager Free 60 (HFP + A2DP + GATT) which has more profiles to tear down. Voyager Legend 50 (HFP + A2DP) should behave similarly to Sync 20. No device should show stale 'Connected' state. Auto-reconnect behavior must NOT occur — the app must wait for explicit user reconnection. Document any device-specific timing differences in disconnect detection.

title: TC-DISCONNECT-006: Immediate State Trigger — Disconnect and Force-Kill App Before Returning
steps: Precondition: Device connected. App open on Device screen.
1. Navigate to: Android Settings → Bluetooth → Paired Devices → Tap gear icon → 'Disconnect'.
2. IMMEDIATELY (within 1 second of tapping Disconnect) force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app via Recent Apps → Swipe away.
3. Wait 10 seconds.
4. Relaunch the Poly Studio Mobile app from the launcher.
5. Observe the connection status on the main Device screen.
6. Tap into device detail screen and verify audio controls state.
expected: 🧠 IMMEDIATE STATE TRIGGER: App must show 'Disconnected' on fresh launch. The app must read the current Bluetooth adapter state on startup, not rely on cached state from the previous session. Actual (If Bug): App shows 'Connected' because it restored a stale persisted state from before the disconnect, or because the disconnect broadcast was never processed before the app was killed. All audio controls must be disabled.

title: TC-DISCONNECT-007: Disconnect via Bluetooth Settings While App is on a Sub-Screen (EQ Settings) — State on Return
steps: Precondition: Device connected. App open.
1. Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer.
2. While on the EQ screen, press Home to background the app.
3. Navigate to: Android Settings → Bluetooth → Paired Devices → Tap gear icon → 'Disconnect'.
4. Return to Poly Studio Mobile app via launcher.
5. Observe which screen the app shows (EQ settings or main Device screen).
6. Verify connection status and EQ control availability.
expected: 🧠 LIFECYCLE DIMENSION GAP: App should return to the EQ screen (maintaining navigation stack) but must show all controls as disabled/greyed out with a 'Disconnected' banner or indicator. Actual (If Bug): App either crashes, resets to main screen losing navigation state, or shows EQ controls as still adjustable despite the device being disconnected. The EQ sliders must be non-interactive when disconnected.

title: TC-DISCONNECT-008: Disconnect via Bluetooth Settings — Verify No Phantom Call Audio Routing
steps: Precondition: Device connected. Poly Studio Mobile app open.
1. Navigate to: Android Settings → Bluetooth → Paired Devices → Tap gear icon → 'Disconnect'.
2. Return to app. Verify 'Disconnected' status.
3. Initiate a phone call (dial any number).
4. During the call, observe audio output device (phone earpiece/speaker vs. Poly device).
5. End the call.
6. Verify Poly device remains disconnected in the app.
expected: 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE VALIDATION: Call audio must route to phone earpiece/speaker, NOT to the disconnected Poly device. Actual (If Bug): Call audio routes to the Poly device even though Bluetooth shows disconnected, because the app's stale 'Connected' state caused the system to maintain an audio route to the device. After call ends, app must still show 'Disconnected' — the call must not trigger an automatic reconnection.
